episodes.title: I Escaped from a Cult - with Sarah LaFever
episodes.description:
Escaping a Cult and Rebuilding a Life | Sarah Le Fever’s Story of Trauma, Survival & Healing

In this deeply emotional and eye-opening conversation, Dr. Todd sits down with Sarah Le Fever, who spent nearly 20 years inside a cult before making the difficult decision to escape and rebuild her life from the ground up. Together, they explore the profound effects of childhood trauma, emotional neglect, abusive environments, cult indoctrination, identity loss, and the long road toward healing and self-discovery.

Sarah shares her experiences growing up in unstable foster and church homes after her mother became seriously ill, including the emotional and physical abuse she endured as a child. She explains how those early experiences created a deep longing for belonging, family, safety, and purpose—needs that ultimately made the cult’s sense of community incredibly appealing.

The conversation dives into:

Childhood trauma and the psychological impact of feeling like a burden
Why vulnerable people are often drawn into cults and high-control groups
The emotional appeal of belonging, certainty, and “family”
How coercive communities slowly reshape identity and autonomy
Emotional abuse, manipulation, and control within cult dynamics
PTSD, fear, hypervigilance, and the struggle to trust yourself again
Escaping an abusive environment with children
Re-entering normal society after years of isolation
Learning how to date, work, communicate, and parent outside the cult
Rebuilding identity after trauma and starting life over

Sarah’s story is both heartbreaking and inspiring. This conversation offers important insight into trauma psychology, cult recovery, emotional abuse, resilience, and the human need for connection and meaning.

If you’ve ever struggled with trauma, controlling relationships, religious abuse, or rebuilding your life after difficult experiences, this episode will resonate deeply.

Topics Covered

00:00 – Introduction to Sarah’s story
01:00 – Childhood trauma and unstable foster/church homes
06:30 – Feeling like a burden and its lifelong effects
10:30 – Searching for belonging and finding the cult
14:00 – How cults create identity and purpose
22:00 – The gradual awakening inside the cult
27:00 – Emotional abuse and fear within marriage
36:00 – Planning and executing the escape
42:00 – Rebuilding life after leaving the cult
47:00 – Learning how to function in normal society again
52:00 – Rethinking parenting, identity, and freedom
